questions.question: The causative agent of malaria is:

options.B: Plasmodium species (P. vivax, P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale)

questions.question: Typhoid fever is caused by:
The primary immune response produces mainly which antibody class?
BCG vaccine protects against:
Which cells are responsible for cell-mediated immunity?
Oncogenes are:

options.B: Mutated proto-oncogenes that drive uncontrolled cell division

questions.question: The diagram shows the structure of an antibody. Which region is responsible for antigen binding?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of IgG antibody showing: Y-shaped structure with two heavy chains and two light chains linked by disulfide bonds. Variable regions (VH and VL) at tips of Y forming two identical antigen-binding sites (Fab regions). Constant region (Fc) at stem interacts with complement and effector cells. Hinge region for flexibility. CDR (complementarity determining regions) at variable tips labeled as antigen binding sites

questions.question: Widal test is used to diagnose:
Which cancer is linked to HPV infection?
The vector for malaria is:
The diagram shows the life cycle of Plasmodium. In which organ does the exo-erythrocytic cycle occur?
questions.diagram_description: Life cycle of Plasmodium: Infected female Anopheles mosquito injects sporozoites into human blood → sporozoites travel to liver (exo-erythrocytic stage: liver cells invaded, merozoites produced) → merozoites enter RBCs (erythrocytic cycle: ring stage, trophozoite, schizont, merozoites released, some become gametocytes) → gametocytes taken up by mosquito (sexual cycle in mosquito gut: gametes, zygote, oocyst, sporozoites)

questions.question: Passive immunity is:

options.B: Immunity obtained by receiving ready-made antibodies (e.g., mother to fetus, antiserum)

questions.question: Which drug is derived from opium poppy and is highly addictive?
Cancer cells differ from normal cells by:

options.B: Uncontrolled proliferation and ability to metastasize

questions.question: The complement system is part of:
The diagram shows the immune response to a vaccine. What is the significance of the secondary response?
questions.diagram_description: Graph showing antibody titre vs time: Primary response after first antigen exposure (slow rise over 2 weeks, lower peak, mostly IgM). Memory cells formed. Secondary response after second antigen exposure (same or booster vaccine) – faster (within days), higher antibody titre, longer lasting, mainly IgG. This is basis of booster doses in vaccination. Memory B and T cells shown persisting between exposures

options.A: Secondary response is weaker than primary
options.B: Secondary response is faster and stronger due to immunological memory cells
options.D: Secondary response requires a new vaccine

questions.question: Metastasis refers to:

options.A: Origin of cancer in one organ
options.B: Spread of cancer cells from primary site to distant organs
options.D: Rapid cell division at one site only

questions.question: Interferons are:

options.B: Proteins secreted by virus-infected cells to protect neighbouring cells

questions.question: Cocaine acts by:
Autoimmune diseases occur when:

options.A: Body fails to respond to pathogens
options.B: Immune system attacks body's own cells and tissues

questions.question: The diagram shows different types of T cells and their functions. Which T cell directly kills infected cells?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of T cell types: CD4+ T helper cells (recognise MHC II, secrete cytokines, help B cells and CTLs), CD8+ Cytotoxic T lymphocytes/CTL (recognise MHC I on infected cells, release perforin and granzymes killing target cell), T regulatory cells (suppress excessive immune response), Memory T cells (long-lived, rapid secondary response). Each cell type with function arrows shown

questions.question: Pneumonia is most commonly caused by:
Tobacco smoking increases risk of:

options.B: Lung cancer, COPD, cardiovascular disease, oral/throat cancers

questions.question: The dengue fever vector is:
Tumour suppressor genes, when mutated, lead to:

options.B: Loss of cell cycle regulation promoting cancer

questions.question: The diagram shows the antigen-antibody interaction and agglutination. What type of immunity is this?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram showing: Antigen (bacterial surface) binding to specific antibody (IgG or IgM). Multiple antibody-antigen complexes forming crosslinks between bacteria → agglutination (clumping). Complement activation shown following antibody binding → membrane attack complex → lysis. Opsonization shown (antibody Fc region recognized by macrophage Fc receptors → phagocytosis enhanced). Labels on each step

questions.question: Lymphoma is cancer of:
Natural killer (NK) cells are part of:

options.B: Innate immunity (kill infected and cancer cells without prior sensitization)

questions.question: The drug used as treatment for malaria is:
Carcinogens are substances that:

options.B: Cause cancer by inducing DNA mutations

questions.question: The diagram shows malaria symptoms timeline. The 48–72 hour cycle of fever corresponds to which event?
questions.diagram_description: Timeline diagram of malaria cycle: Sporozoite enters liver (10–14 day incubation, no symptoms). Merozoites enter RBCs. In RBC: ring stage → trophozoite → schizont → rupture releases merozoites + pyrogens every 48 hrs (P. vivax, P. falciparum – tertian malaria) or 72 hrs (P. malariae – quartan malaria). Graph showing fever spikes every 48 or 72 hours corresponding to synchronized RBC rupture

options.B: Rupture of infected RBCs releasing merozoites and pyrogens

questions.question: Heroin (diacetylmorphine) is obtained from:
MHC (Major Histocompatibility Complex) molecules are important for:
Radiotherapy for cancer works by:

options.B: Damaging DNA of rapidly dividing cancer cells

questions.question: Memory cells in the immune system:

options.B: Persist long-term and enable faster secondary immune response

questions.question: Alcohol abuse damages which organ primarily?
ELISA (Enzyme Linked Immunosorbent Assay) is used to detect:
Leukaemia is cancer of:

options.B: Blood cells (WBCs, uncontrolled proliferation of immature blood cells)

questions.question: Which type of hypersensitivity is anaphylaxis (Type I)?

options.B: IgE-mediated (mast cell degranulation causing immediate hypersensitivity)

questions.question: The anti-retroviral therapy (ART) for HIV works by:

options.B: Inhibiting reverse transcriptase, protease, integrase (reducing viral replication)

questions.question: The diagram shows cancer development stages. What is the term for transformation of normal cell into cancer cell?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram of cancer progression: Normal cell → DNA damage by carcinogen/mutation → mutated cell (proto-oncogene becomes oncogene or tumour suppressor mutated) → malignant transformation (loss of contact inhibition, immortalization) → primary tumour (benign stage, contained) → invasion of basement membrane → metastasis (cancer cells in lymph or blood vessels, colonize distant organs). Each stage labeled with molecular changes

questions.question: Opioids cause dependence by:

options.B: Binding mu-opioid receptors causing euphoria and physical dependence

questions.question: Which vitamin deficiency increases susceptibility to infections?

options.B: Vitamin A (maintains integrity of epithelial barriers and immune function)

questions.question: Benign tumour differs from malignant tumour in that it:

options.B: Does not invade surrounding tissues or metastasize

questions.question: The Mantoux test (tuberculin test) detects:

options.B: Prior exposure to Mycobacterium tuberculosis (delayed hypersensitivity reaction)

questions.question: The diagram shows how vaccines work. Which cells are responsible for immunological memory?
questions.diagram_description: Vaccine mechanism diagram: Vaccine antigen (attenuated pathogen/toxoid/subunit) injected → APC (dendritic cells) present antigen via MHC → Naive B and T cells activated → clonal expansion → effector cells (plasma cells secreting antibodies, cytotoxic T cells) + Memory B cells and Memory T cells (long-lived, dormant). Second exposure to pathogen → rapid activation of memory cells → faster, larger immune response

options.B: Memory B cells and Memory T cells

questions.question: Cannabis (marijuana) contains which psychoactive substance?
Which of the following is a carcinoma?
Immunodeficiency diseases result from:

options.B: Deficiency or dysfunction of one or more immune system components

questions.question: Congenital immunity is also called:
